heroText: Mbabane is a capital city in Eswatini, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Mbabane
highlights.text: Mbabane is a capital city in Eswatini, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.text: Mbabane was founded in 1887 by Mickey Wells, on the spot where the Transvaal-to-Mozambique route crossed the Mbabane river.
The town grew after the nation's administrative centre was moved from Bremersdorp (now called Manzini) in January 1902.

history.label: 1930s
history.text: By the 1930s, Mbabane had electricity, running water, telephone connection and a hospital.

history.label: Growth
history.text: It derives its name from a chief, Mbabane Kunene, who lived in the area when some British settlers arrived.

history.label: Civic role
history.text: During this time, Mbabane consisted of a few shops, churches and schools founded by white settlers.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The black Swazi Africans were not allowed to live in the town and had to reside in nearby rural districts.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Prior to WW2, most Swazis lived in rural districts and worked outside Eswatini, so there was little growth in the town.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: In the years following independence, governmental buildings such as the British Consulate were built in Mbabane.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Growth has occurred with the tourism industry in Eswatini, of which Mbabane has become the centre.
